reasoning: The ICE BofA US Corporate Index Option-Adjusted Spread is currently indicated to be at 0.760 (76 basis points) in the prompt's context. This is near the historical absolute minimum for investment-grade corporate credit spreads in the US, which typically floor around 70-80 bps. Because credit spreads exhibit significant mean-reverting behavior and are bounded at zero (but practically bounded near 0.70-0.80), the spread is overwhelmingly more likely to experience an increase than a decrease over most medium to long-term intervals. Assuming typical time horizons tied to the question close date of February 2026, an upward move is favored strongly by current tight valuations.
